description: Formal objects of category theory, used as the reflexive meta-domain in KOS. KOS uses categorical structures as its transfer infrastructure; this domain represents those structures as knowledge objects within KOS itself.
grounding_notes: Category theory sources are classical mathematics literature. Olog applications from Spivak & Kent 2012 (PLoS ONE). KOS transfer validity mechanism is a derived formalization, not a formal proof.

attributes.contribution: co-invented category theory with Mac Lane

attributes.definition: A collection of objects and morphisms between them, with composition and identities
An element of a category with no required internal structure
attributes.note: Opacity is intentional — what matters is the morphisms, not the object's internals

attributes.definition: An arrow f: A → B in a category
attributes.note: Morphisms are the primary structure; objects are just anchors for morphisms

attributes.definition: A structure-preserving map between categories: maps objects to objects, morphisms to morphisms, preserves composition and identity
attributes.in_kos: AlignmentMap is a partial functor between local ontologies

attributes.definition: A family of morphisms η_X: F(X) → G(X) for functors F,G: C → D, natural in X
attributes.key_property: naturality squares commute: η_Y ∘ F(f) = G(f) ∘ η_X
attributes.in_kos: NaturalTransformationCandidate — compatibility between two alignment maps

attributes.definition: A pair of functors F: C → D and G: D → C with a natural bijection Hom(F(A), B) ≅ Hom(A, G(B))
attributes.intuition: F is left adjoint to G; F is the 'freest' construction, G is the 'cofree' one

attributes.definition: Terminal cone over a diagram: the most general object with maps to all objects in the diagram
attributes.in_kos: Intersection of two AlignmentMaps — the common core of two domain alignments

attributes.definition: Initial cocone under a diagram: dual to limit
attributes.in_kos: Minimal merge of two domain alignments — the GlobalGluingGap records where this fails

attributes.definition: A specific limit: given f: A→C and g: B→C, the pullback A×_C B is the universal object with maps to A and B that agree on C
attributes.intuition: The 'intersection' or 'common fiber' of two structures over a base
attributes.in_kos: Shared subgraph when two domains are both aligned to a third

attributes.definition: Dual of pullback: given f: C→A and g: C→B, the pushout A+_C B is the universal object with maps from A and B that agree on C
attributes.intuition: The 'merge' of two structures sharing a common substructure
attributes.in_kos: Minimal combined domain when two domains share structural elements

attributes.statement: For any functor F: C → Set and object A in C: Nat(Hom(A,−), F) ≅ F(A)
attributes.intuition: An object is completely determined by the morphisms into it — 'to know an object is to know its relationships'
attributes.significance: The most important lemma in category theory: morphisms are the primary epistemic access

attributes.definition: A functor satisfying the gluing axioms: local sections that agree on overlaps can be uniquely glued into a global section
attributes.in_kos: Knowledge consistency condition: local knowledge in each domain can be glued into a global knowledge state if and only if the domains are coherent on their overlaps

mechanisms.name: Functor Composition
mechanisms.notes: Composition of structure-preserving maps is structure-preserving

decisions.question: Should KOS use category theory as its transfer infrastructure?
decisions.outcome: Yes — AlignmentMap as partial functor, NaturalTransformationCandidate for compatibility between translations
decisions.rationale: Category theory is the mathematics of structure-preserving maps. The transfer problem in KOS is exactly the problem of finding structure-preserving maps between local ontologies. No alternative framework captures the composability, the loss accounting (partial functors), and the upgrade path (natural transformations) in a unified way.

evidence.content_ref: Spivak & Kent (2012) - Ologs: A Categorical Framework for Knowledge Representation
evidence.notes: Demonstrates that ontologies can be formalized as categories with functors as translations between them

evidence.content_ref: Fong & Spivak (2018) - Seven Sketches in Compositionality
evidence.notes: Applied category theory for scientists — the standard reference for CT in knowledge systems

bridge_candidates.claim: KOS AlignmentMap is an instantiation of a partial functor in the category-theoretic sense
bridge_candidates.structural_loss: KOS AlignmentMap is partial (gaps allowed); strict functors require total maps. The loss is documented in GlobalGluingGap.
